summary: The image is a well-known artistic/scientific illustration of the observable universe rendered in a logarithmic, spherical projection. It depicts cosmic structures from the Solar System at the center outward through stars, nebulae, galaxies, and the cosmic microwave background at the outer edge. This is a cosmological visualization, not a biotech or science project document, and contains no experimental data, therapeutic targets, clinical or preclinical results, IP, regulatory, or funding information relevant to a TRL assessment.

content_blocks.title: Logarithmic Map of the Observable Universe
content_blocks.content: A circular, logarithmic-scale artistic illustration of the entire observable universe. The center shows the Solar System (Sun and planets visible), surrounded by concentric regions representing: the Milky Way galaxy, nearby galaxies (e.g., Andromeda), galaxy clusters, large-scale cosmic structure (filaments and voids), and finally the cosmic microwave background (CMB) glowing in orange/red at the outermost ring. The image is purely cosmological/astronomical in nature and contains no biotech, pharmaceutical, or clinical content.

key_findings: This document is a cosmological illustration (logarithmic map of the observable universe) with no relevance to biotech, drug development, or TRL assessment.
No experimental data, model systems, therapeutic targets, regulatory interactions, IP, or funding information are present.
The image is likely the well-known artwork by Pablo Carlos Budassi or a similar artist depicting the universe on a logarithmic scale.
